project: VERA: Verified Erasure under Reweighting Ambiguity
scope: Secondary theory and analysis extension; it does not change any dataset, eraser, seed, candidate, attacker, threshold, validation fraction, split, or primary deployment rule in the parent preregistration.
motivation: A single common Gamma hides which supported environment limits a certificate and treats unsupported environments only as an all-or-nothing boundary. The extension reports the full groupwise certificate from the same registered contracts.

object.name: support-aware erasure shift envelope
object.definition: For every edit and observed environment g, lower-certify the largest within-environment density-ratio budget Gamma_g for which that environment's paired target-harm contract and every registered environment/source/attacker leakage contract hold. A deployment mixture is inside the envelope when it assigns mass only to observed environments whose requested Gamma_g does not exceed the corresponding certified radius.
object.common_radius_relation: For any declared supported environment set, the original common shift radius is the minimum groupwise radius over that set.
object.unsupported_rule: A registered environment absent from certification receives radius zero under the nonparametric model. Any positive certificate for it requires separately stated cross-environment structure or representative data.

theory.simultaneous_coverage: Use the parent study's Bonferroni family over every candidate and every target/leakage contract. Computing groupwise radii is post-processing of the same simultaneous confidence event and spends no additional error probability.
theory.mixture_guarantee: On the simultaneous event, every arbitrary mixture of allowed within-group deployment laws satisfies all registered contracts.
theory.sharp_boundary: The unsupported-cell two-world construction proves that no validation-only protocol can uniformly assign a nonzero radius to an unobserved required cell in the nonparametric model while retaining false-acceptance control.

registered_outputs: Per-candidate JSON map from observed environment to certified radius.
Per-candidate list of unsupported registered external environments with radius zero.
Numerical audit that the common radius equals the minimum observed groupwise radius to bisection tolerance when no forced unsupported-environment abstention applies.
A support-envelope figure showing groupwise radii and zero-radius unsupported groups without using external outcomes for selection or tuning.

analysis_constraints.claim_status: Secondary confirmatory extension for the certificate geometry; external benchmark comparisons remain governed by the parent preregistration and correction ledger.

pass_conditions.source_and_hash: This JSON and its SHA-256 sidecar must match.
pass_conditions.theorem: The manuscript and supplement state and prove the simultaneous groupwise envelope corollary and its relation to the common radius.
pass_conditions.implementation: Independent tests compare groupwise and joint production certificates on exhaustive discrete examples and randomized receipt-like arrays.
pass_conditions.support: Camelyon17-WILDS center 2 is reported with radius zero and is never described as a measured contract failure solely because it is unsupported.
pass_conditions.provenance: All derived rows retain the parent preregistration hash and official receipt hashes.
